method: Put all the ingredients except the oil into the liquidiser and beat for about 1 minute.
Add the oil drop by drop through the hole in the lid, starting with a very slow steady stream.
Continue until the mixture thickens and will not take any more oil.
If needed, stir in a little oil by hand and liquidise again.

title: Lemon Mayonnaise
description: A lemon-flavoured mayonnaise variation.
method: Prepare in the same way as the mayonnaise.
Use lemon juice and lemon rind instead of wine vinegar and garlic.

title: Low Fat Lemon Mayonnaise
description: Lemon mayonnaise finished with low fat fromage frais.
ingredients: 3 tbsp low fat fromage frais, or to taste
method: Prepare as for lemon mayonnaise.
Fold in the fromage frais at the end to taste.

title: Salsa Verde
description: A herb sauce with parsley, basil, mint, anchovies, capers and olive oil.
method: Pick the parsley leaves from the stalks and put them into a food processor.
Add the basil, mint, mustard, anchovies, capers and 2 tbsp of the oil.
Crack and peel the garlic, remove the green shoot, chop and crush it with a generous pinch of salt.
Add the garlic to the processor.
Process for a couple of minutes, scraping down the bowl as needed.
With the machine running, add the rest of the oil in a thin stream.
Season with pepper.
Cover and chill until required.
notes: The finished sauce should look like a coarse green mayonnaise.

title: Lamb Seasoning
description: A dry herb seasoning mix for lamb.
method: Mix all the ingredients together.
Sprinkle over lamb before cooking.

title: Chestnut and Sausage Stuffing
description: A rich stuffing with chestnuts, chestnut purée, sausage meat, celery and breadcrumbs.
prepTime: Overnight soaking if using dried chestnuts
ingredients: 1 lb whole chestnuts, fresh or dried
1 pint veal stock or chicken stock
method: If using dried chestnuts, cover with water and soak overnight, then remove any remaining inner skins.
If using fresh chestnuts, remove the shells, blanch and peel off the inner skins.
Place the chestnuts in a saucepan with enough stock to cover, the chopped celery and seasoning.
Cook until the chestnuts are very tender and all the stock has been absorbed.
Tip into a bowl to cool.
Chop the onion finely and cook in the butter until soft.
When cool, add the onion to the sausage meat.
Work in the chestnuts and enough breadcrumbs to bind.
notes: If omitting the chestnuts, cook the celery in stock until tender or omit the celery entirely if preferred.

title: Apple Tuiles / Apple Crisps
description: Thin apple slices dipped in syrup and dried slowly until crisp.
ingredients: For the stock syrup: 1 1/4 lb sugar
For the stock syrup: 1 3/4 pints water
For the stock syrup: grated rind of 1 lemon
For the tuiles: 2 Granny Smith apples
For the tuiles: juice of 1/2 lemon
method: Dissolve the sugar in the water.
Add the lemon rind and boil for 5 minutes, then allow to cool.
Mix the lemon juice with the syrup.
Core the apples and slice them very thinly.
Dip the apple slices in the syrup.
Place on parchment paper in the lowest oven for about 2 hours.
Remove when they start to wrinkle slightly and look a little dry.
Wrap quickly around a rolling pin if you want them curved.
notes: Other flavourings such as star anise or cinnamon stick can be used in the syrup.

title: Beetroot Horseradish
description: A beetroot and horseradish relish stored in a jar with diluted acetic acid.
method: Clean and grate the horseradish.
Cook the beetroot until tender, then grate and add it to the horseradish.
Add sugar and salt to taste and mix well.
Put the mixture into a screw-topped jar.
Make a hole in the centre and add the diluted acetic acid.
Mix well.

title: Melba Toast
description: Very crisp thin toast made from sliced white bread.
ingredients: 1 large white loaf of medium sliced bread
method: Cut the crusts from each slice of bread.
Toast the slices in a toaster.
When toasted, cut through each slice so you have two thin squares with one side toasted and the other not.
Place the slices under a grill and toast until golden.
notes: Watch carefully, as they burn very easily.

title: Red Onion Marmalade
description: A sweet-sour onion relish for roast meats, pickles or chutney-style serving.
method: Gently sauté the onions in the oil for about 10 minutes until soft but not coloured.
Add the remaining ingredients.
Simmer uncovered for about 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
Increase the heat towards the end if needed to boil off excess liquid.
notes: Keeps refrigerated for several days or longer.
Can be served hot with roast meat or cold like a chutney.

title: Rum Pot
description: A long-maturing preserved fruit mixture built up gradually with rum.
method: Clean a large glass jar.
Wash and hull the strawberries.
Sprinkle generously with castor sugar and leave overnight.
Add the strawberries to the rum pot and cover with rum.
Store in a dark place.
Continue with other prepared and sugared fruits as they come into season, ending with plums.
Always cover the fruit with rum after adding it.
Leave for 6 months after adding the last fruit.

title: Yorkshire Pudding
description: Traditional Yorkshire pudding cooked in very hot fat until crisp and risen.
method: Sift the flour into a bowl and make a well in the centre.
Break the egg into the well and beat it, gradually incorporating the flour, milk, water and seasoning.
Heat the oven to 425°F / Gas 7 / 220°C.
Put the roasting tin containing the dripping on a baking sheet and heat for 15 minutes.
Remove the tin and place it over direct heat while pouring the batter into the hot, sizzling fat.
Return to the oven on the top shelf or just below.
Cook for 25-30 minutes until risen, crisp and golden.
Serve as soon as possible.
notes: The source stresses three rules: very hot oven, a flameproof metal container, and plain flour rather than self-raising.

title: Minestrone Soup
description: A quick minestrone-style soup with consommé, beans, vegetables and macaroni.
ingredients: 1 medium carrot, peeled and finely diced
1 medium leek, trimmed, cut into 5cm lengths and shredded
1 small garlic clove, peeled and crushed
1 stick celery, scraped and finely diced
1 small onion, peeled and finely chopped
method: Combine the consommé, boiling water, beans, carrot, leek, macaroni, tomatoes, garlic, celery, onion, ham if using, salt, pepper and 2 tsp of the Parmesan in a large microwave-safe bowl.
Three-quarters cover with cling film and cook on maximum for 20 minutes, stirring twice through the gap during cooking.
Leave to stand for 5 minutes before carefully removing the cling film.
Serve hot, sprinkling over the remaining Parmesan.
notes: Source specifies microwave setting: maximum (full).

title: Thai Chicken and Coconut Soup
description: A fragrant Thai-style soup with chicken stock, ginger, lime, lemongrass, sweetcorn and coriander.
cookTime: About 10 minutes plus a few minutes finishing
ingredients: Ginger, a thumb-sized piece, peeled and grated
1-2 lemongrass sticks, chopped and crushed, tied in muslin
method: Prepare the chicken stock.
Add the ginger, lime juice, lemongrass bag, chilli oil and Worcestershire sauce if using.
Heat the stock and flavourings for about 10 minutes.
Add the sweetcorn, coriander, red pepper and most of the spring onions, then cook for a few minutes.
Add the remaining spring onions at the end if you like.
Adjust the seasoning to taste and serve.
notes: The source title includes coconut, but the source ingredient list does not include coconut milk or cream.

title: Leek and Potato Soup
description: A simple blended soup made with leek, potato, butter and stock.
method: Heat the butter slowly in a pan until melted.
Add the chopped leek and cook gently for 5 minutes.
Add the potatoes, cover, and cook slowly for another 5-10 minutes until softened.
Pour in the stock and bring to the boil.
Simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Blend until smooth and serve hot.
notes: The source title appears as 'Leel and Potato Soup'; standardised here to 'Leek and Potato Soup'.

title: Avocado Israeli Style
description: A very simple avocado starter mashed with onion and lemon juice.
method: Mash the avocado.
Mix with chopped onion and lemon juice to taste.
Serve as a starter.
notes: The source does not give exact quantities.

title: Chopped Herrings
description: Minced rollmops with onion, egg, apple, breadcrumbs, vinegar and sugar.
ingredients: 1 slice of bread, or enough to make the required consistency
method: Skin the herrings, removing the back fin if you like, and rinse briefly under water.
Peel the onion and apple.
Put the herrings, onion, apple and eggs through a mincer.
Mince the bread last and add half of it to the mixture.
Add the vinegar and sugar and mix well.
Add more breadcrumbs if needed to reach the consistency you want.
Adjust the sugar or vinegar to taste before serving.

title: Greek Yogurt Coleslaw
description: A lighter coleslaw made with Greek yogurt, cider vinegar and cabbage.
ingredients: 16 oz / about 6 1/4 cups bagged coleslaw blend or shredded cabbage
method: In a small bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, vinegar, sugar, salt and pepper and whisk until smooth.
Place the coleslaw blend or shredded cabbage in a large bowl and pour the dressing over it.
Stir well to coat thoroughly.
Cover and refrigerate until using, preferably for at least 30 minutes.

title: The Perfect Red Cabbage
description: A spiced red cabbage with apple, balsamic vinegar and cranberry sauce.
ingredients: 1 red cabbage, cored and cut into irregular chunks
method: Melt the butter in a large pan over a medium heat and add the onion.
Soften for a few minutes, then stir in the cinnamon, cloves and nutmeg and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cabbage and sauté until shiny and well coated.
Add the apple, sugar and vinegar, reduce the heat to low, stir well, cover and cook for 45 minutes, stirring occasionally so it does not stick.
Stir in the cranberry sauce and cook for another 25 minutes.
Season well and stir through an extra knob of butter before serving.
notes: Can be made a couple of days ahead and reheated before serving.

title: Crock Pot Baked Spiced Red Cabbage With Apples or Pears
description: Slow-cooked red cabbage with onions, warm spices, and apples or pears.
cookTime: 6-12 hours in a crock pot, or about 2-3 hours conventionally
ingredients: 4 large onions, peeled and finely diced
4 large apples or 4 large pears, unpeeled and finely diced
4 garlic cloves, peeled and finely minced
method: Discard the outer leaves of the cabbage, cut into quarters, remove the woody core and shred finely.
Cut the apples or pears into quarters, core them and dice finely.
Place the cabbage in the crock pot, then season with salt and pepper.
Layer over the apples or pears, onions and garlic, then add the spices and brown sugar.
Continue layering in the same way until everything has been used.
Dot the top with the butter and pour over the red wine vinegar.
Season once more with salt and pepper.
Cook on high in the crock pot for 6-12 hours until soft, rich and deep red.
notes: Can also be cooked in a conventional casserole at 180°C / 360°F for about 2-2½ hours, or on the hob for about 2-3 hours on a low simmer.

title: Marrow Cream (Marrow Lemon Curd)
description: A preserve-style lemon curd made with marrow instead of eggs.
method: Steam the marrow until tender.
Strain well and leave to drain overnight, or dry in a salad spinner.
Mash or liquidise to a smooth purée.
Put the purée into a preserving pan with the butter, sugar, and the grated rind and juice of the lemons.
Heat gently, stirring, until all the sugar has dissolved.
Boil for 20 minutes, stirring all the time.
Spoon into sterilised jars and seal.
notes: The source describes this as lemon curd made with marrow instead of eggs.
The mixture can spit while boiling, so use a deep preserving pan or pasta pot.

title: Caramelised Onion and Wild Mushroom Tart
description: A savoury tart with balsamic onions, wild mushrooms and a light fromage frais filling.
ingredients: 1 x 500g tub virtually fat free fromage frais
method: Roll out the pastry and line a 23cm loose-bottomed tart tin.
Chill for 20 minutes.
Heat half the olive oil in a frying pan, add the onions and fry gently for about 20 minutes until softened.
Increase the heat and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring regularly, until lightly browned.
Stir in the sugar and balsamic vinegar and simmer for 5 minutes until the mixture is jammy, then set aside.
Prick the pastry base, line with greaseproof paper and fill with baking beans.
Bake at 200°C / 400°F / Gas 6 for 15 minutes, then remove the paper and beans and bake for another 5 minutes.
Heat the remaining oil in a frying pan and fry the mushrooms and garlic for about 5 minutes, stirring.
Stir in the parsley, then drain off any excess liquid.
Spread the onion mixture in the pastry case, then spread over the mushrooms and garlic.
Whisk together the fromage frais, eggs, salt and pepper and pour into the pastry case.
Bake at 190°C / 375°F / Gas 5 for 20-25 minutes until golden brown and set.
Cool slightly before serving.

title: Jamie’s Fantastic Fish Pie
description: A hearty fish pie with spinach, hard-boiled eggs, creamy vegetables and mashed potato.
ingredients: Mature Cheddar or Parmesan cheese, grated
450g haddock or cod fillet, skin off and pin-boned
method: Preheat the oven to 230°C / 450°F / Gas 8.
Peel and dice the potatoes into 2.5cm chunks, then cook in boiling salted water for 2 minutes.
Carefully add the eggs to the pan and cook for a further 8 minutes, by which time the potatoes should also be cooked.
Steam the spinach in a colander over the pan for about 1 minute, then squeeze out any excess moisture.
Drain the potatoes.
Cool the eggs under cold water, peel them and cut into quarters.
Peel and finely chop the onion, then peel, halve and finely chop the carrot.
In a separate pan, fry the onion and carrot slowly in a little olive oil for about 5 minutes.
Add the double cream and bring just to the boil.
Remove from the heat, grate in 2 good handfuls of cheese, squeeze in the lemon juice and stir through the mustard.
Finely chop the parsley and stir it into the cream mixture.
Slice the fish into strips and place in an ovenproof dish with the spinach and eggs.
Pour over the creamy vegetable sauce.
Mash the potatoes with a little olive oil, sea salt, black pepper and nutmeg if using.
Spread the mash over the fish pie filling.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden on top.
notes: The source suggests serving with peas or greens.

title: Apricot-glazed Chicken with Peaches and Raisins
description: Chicken baked with dried peaches and raisins, then glazed with apricot jam and white wine.
ingredients: 2 x 3 lb chickens, cut into serving pieces
method: Season the chicken with salt and garlic to taste.
Place the dried peaches and raisins in the bottom of a greased baking tray.
Arrange the chicken pieces on top.
Brush with the melted butter and drizzle over 3/4 cup of the white wine.
Cover loosely with foil and bake at 325°F for about 45 minutes for chicken pieces, or about 30 minutes for breasts.
Warm the apricot jam in a small saucepan and sieve it back into the pan.
Stir in the remaining white wine.
Uncover the chicken and baste with the apricot and wine glaze.
Continue baking uncovered, basting frequently, until the chicken is tender and deeply glazed.
Garnish with parsley before serving.
notes: The source notes that if the chicken is pre-baked and refrigerated, the final uncovered cooking time may need to be increased.

title: Salchicha Pasta
description: A simple pasta dish with chipolata sausages, chopped tomatoes, baked beans and barbecue sauce.
cookTime: About 10 minutes plus pasta cooking time
method: Bring a saucepan of water to the boil for the pasta.
Slice each chipolata into short pieces about 1-1.5cm long.
Chop the onion finely.
Heat the oil in a pan and fry the sausage pieces until browned on two sides.
Add the onion and garlic and cook until softened.
Cook the pasta while the sauce is cooking.
Add the chopped tomatoes and baked beans to the pan and stir well.
Add a large squirt of barbecue mop sauce and reduce the heat.
Cook on a low heat for about 10 minutes.
Drain the pasta and add it to the sauce.
Stir thoroughly and serve.
notes: The source suggests serving with fresh challah.

title: Aubergine, Tomato and Parmesan Bake
description: A layered baked aubergine dish with tomato sauce, basil, Parmesan and egg.
ingredients: 85g Parmesan, or vegetarian alternative, freshly grated
method: Heat the oven to 200°C / Fan 180°C / Gas 6.
In a shallow pan, cook the garlic in 4 tbsp of the olive oil over a fairly high heat for 3 minutes.
Add the tomatoes and simmer for 8 minutes, stirring now and then.
Stir in the tomato purée.
Heat a griddle pan until very hot.
Brush some of the aubergine slices with a little oil and griddle them for 5-7 minutes until browned and cooked through, turning halfway.
Lift onto kitchen paper and repeat with the remaining aubergines.
Lay a few aubergine slices in the bottom of an ovenproof dish, then spoon over some tomato sauce.
Sprinkle with Parmesan and basil leaves and season lightly.
Repeat the layers with the remaining ingredients.
Pour the beaten egg over the top and sprinkle with a little more Parmesan.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until the topping is golden.
notes: The source notes that it is even better made a day ahead.

title: Leek and Mushroom Pithivier
description: A puff pastry pie filled with creamed leeks, mushrooms and Gruyère.
cookTime: About 50-60 minutes plus cooling the filling
ingredients: 125g unsalted butter, plus extra for greasing
1 egg yolk beaten with 1 tsp water, for glaze
method: Melt 25g of the butter in a heavy-based pan over a medium heat.
Add the leeks and cook, stirring frequently, for about 2 minutes.
Add another 40g butter and 3 tbsp water, cover, reduce the heat and cook for about 20 minutes until tender, stirring occasionally.
Uncover and cook for 5-7 minutes more until all the moisture has evaporated.
Transfer the leeks to a bowl and leave to cool.
When cool, stir in the crème fraîche and chill until cold.
Melt the remaining butter in a frying pan over a high heat.
Add the mushrooms and cook, tossing frequently, for about 5 minutes until lightly browned.
Season to taste and stir the mushrooms into the leek mixture gently so the leeks keep their shape.
Heat the oven to 200°C / Fan 180°C.
Roll out half the pastry on a lightly floured surface into a 25cm round.
Place it on a greased sheet of greaseproof paper on a baking sheet.
Spread half the leek mixture over the pastry, leaving a 2.5cm border.
Put the Gruyère in the centre, then top with the rest of the leek mixture.
Roll out the remaining pastry into a 33cm round.
Brush the border with egg glaze, place the lid over the filling and crimp the edges firmly with the back of a fork.
Brush the top with egg glaze and make a small steam hole in the centre.
Bake until the pastry is risen, crisp and golden brown.
notes: The source notes that the filling should be cold before the pie is assembled so the pastry stays crisp and flaky.

title: Tinned-tomato Risotto
description: A tomato risotto finished with Parmesan and optional mozzarella, anchovies and parsley oil.
ingredients: 100ml dry white wine or dry vermouth
600ml vegetable stock, or chicken stock if preferred
method: Heat the olive oil and a knob of the butter in a heavy-bottomed pan and sauté the onion gently until soft but not coloured.
Add the garlic and cook for another 4 minutes.
Stir in the risotto rice so it is coated in the fat.
Add the white wine or vermouth and stir until it has reduced almost completely.
Heat the stock in a separate pan until simmering.
Stir the tomatoes into the rice, then add a ladleful of stock.
Once the rice has absorbed the liquid, keep adding more stock a ladleful at a time, stirring continuously, until the rice is cooked but still has a little bite.
Stir in the remaining butter until melted, then add the Parmesan.
Check the seasoning and add pepper if needed.
If using mozzarella, stir it through at the end and allow it to melt slightly.
If using the anchovy and parsley oil, stir the oil, anchovies and parsley together and spoon a little over each serving.
notes: You may need a little more liquid as the risotto cooks; water can be used once the stock runs out.
